headline: Why the Trade Desk Is Losing a Key Partner—and What It Means for the Stock
summary: The Trade Desk  stock was dropping Wednesday after two analysts downgraded the stock following an advertising company’s decision to stop recommending the digital ad platform to customers.  French ad firm  Publicis  told Barron’s that an independent auditor concluded that  The Trade Desk  did not pass an audit, and “as a result of the audit findings we will no longer be recommending The Trade Desk as a solution for our clients.”  “We look forward to working with Publicis to provide workable alternatives to this particular request, including information at an even more granular level than requested,” The Trade Desk added.

French ad firm  Publicis  told Barron’s that an independent auditor concluded that  The Trade Desk  did not pass an audit, and “as a result of the audit findings we will no longer be recommending The Trade Desk as a solution for our clients.”  “We look forward to working with Publicis to provide workable alternatives to this particular request, including information at an even more granular level than requested,” The Trade Desk added.

headline: Trade Desk drops after report that Publicis advised clients against using its platform
summary: Advertising technology firm Trade Desk's shares tumbled on Wednesday as Wall Street analysts downgraded the stock following a report ‌that French ad giant Publicis Groupe advised its clients against using the ‌company's media buying platform.  The stock slid nearly 6%, adding to Tuesday's 7.4% drop after Ad Age ​reported that a recent audit commissioned by Publicis found Trade Desk had violated multiple clauses of their agreement, prompting the adverse recommendation.  Trade Desk charged multiple fees that exceeded the limits of the agreement and opted clients into extra features without their consent, the ‌report said, citing the audit.

raw.headline: Trade Desk drops after report that Publicis advised clients against using its platform
raw.summary: Advertising technology firm Trade Desk's shares tumbled on Wednesday as Wall Street analysts downgraded the stock following a report ‌that French ad giant Publicis Groupe advised its clients against using the ‌company's media buying platform.  The stock slid nearly 6%, adding to Tuesday's 7.4% drop after Ad Age ​reported that a recent audit commissioned by Publicis found Trade Desk had violated multiple clauses of their agreement, prompting the adverse recommendation.  Trade Desk charged multiple fees that exceeded the limits of the agreement and opted clients into extra features without their consent, the ‌report said, citing the audit.

headline: Alibaba Hikes AI Service Prices Up to 34% After Demand Soars
summary: Its storage service, known as Cloud Parallel File Storage, will also cost 30% more, it said.  The price hikes emerged after Alibaba launched a major structural revamp this month to focus on monetizing AI.  Alibaba shares rose as much as 3.2% in Hong Kong on Wednesday.

headline: Cipher Digital Resets With AI Leases While Shares Lag Analyst Targets
summary: Cipher Digital (NasdaqGS:CIFR) has repositioned as an AI and high performance computing, or HPC, infrastructure provider. The company has entered into major long term infrastructure leases with Amazon Web Services and Google. Cipher Digital has completed a corporate rebrand that reflects its focus on AI and HPC infrastructure. Soros Fund Management has made a significant investment in Cipher Digital, providing institutional validation of the new business model. Cipher Digital now operates...
